songs.title: T-T-Turtle
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
t… t… t… t…
(tick, tick, tick)

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
T! T! T-t-t!
What begins with T?
T-t-turtle! T-t-turtle!
It's a turtle! (a turtle!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
T! T! T-t-t!
What begins with T?
T-t-tiger! T-t-tiger!
It's a tiger! (a tiger!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
T! T! T-t-t!
What begins with T?
T-t-tomato! T-t-tomato!
It's a tomato! (a tomato!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
t… t… t…
t-t-taxi… t-t-teddy… t-t-towel…

[Final Hook — big]
T! T! T-t-t!
What begins with T?
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not T!
T-t-table! T-t-table!
It's a table, table, table!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ONE pattern: "T-t-X! It's a X!" — the stutter is the sound isolation, and the answer line REUSES Week 1's frame (It's a ___) so the songs stack like the letters do. The potato tries to sneak in and the class shouts NO — that's initial-sound discrimination disguised as a joke, and it mirrors the mixed-basket I Spy game mid-week. Ends on "table" — a real /t/ thing they can slap on the beat. Stop consonants NEVER sung with schwa.

songs.title: Where Is Segina?
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
t… t… t…
Where is Sejeena?

[Hook 1 — call and response]
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
She's at the park! At the park!
She's at the park, park, park!

[Hook 2 — call and response]
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
She's at school! At school!
She's at school, school, school!

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
t… t… t…
at… at… at…
where is Sejeena…?

[Hook 3 — call and response]
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
She's at the mall! At the mall!
She's at the mall, mall, mall!

[Final Hook — quiet, then big]
Where is Sejeena? (where? where?)
Where is Sejeena? (shhh…)
She's at home. She's in bed.
Sejeena is asleep…
WAKE UP, SEJEENA!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: 🚨 Name spelling: lyrics use 'Sejeena' (phonetic, so Suno pronounces it /se-JEE-na/). Everything PRINTED — book, cards, dictionary, map — uses 'Segina' (her real spelling). ONE pattern: "Where is Segina? She's at the ___." Place swapped per hook. Ends on the book's twist (home, asleep) and a full-class shout. In circle, swap Segina for real children's names and real places — the song is a machine, the class feeds it. Unplugged circle version: same words to Frère Jacques ("Where is Segina? Where is Segina? At the park! At the park!") — the daily map-game soundtrack, no speaker needed.

She's at the park.

spreads.text:

spreads.text:

dark suspense page, text only, no image — whispered by the whole class

spreads.text: She's at home. Shhh. Segina is asleep.
spreads.artNote: Segina asleep in a tiny wooden bed, moonlight through a small window. Someone always yells "WAKE UP SEGINA" here — let them, it's the song link.

extras: Big Map A3 (illustrated town: park, school, mall, police station, zoo, bus stop, supermarket, houses, roads — laminated)
Segina wooden peg doll + 3-4 figures basket

teacherFocus: Land the week's crown moment — sandpaper 'a' + sandpaper 't' slid together into "at", the first word built from taught sounds — then live it all week on the Big Map ("Where is Segina? She's at the ___.") with daily hide-and-seek.
